immediate neighbor of both Alice and Karan.

metals viz., Mg, Br, Fe, Li, Ga, Tc, Cs and Er. The
distance between the two students is equal.
Note: Y is a neighbour of X and Z or Y sits
adjacent to X and Z means Y sits between X
and Y on the same side.
D is the neighbour of the students who have 'Cs'
and 'Tc'. Five students sit between A and D. So,
there are two cases:
One student sits between A and L. L faces the
person who is second to the right of F. E sits
third to the right of A.
I faces outwards and sits fourth to the right of K.
A is adjacent to students who have 'Mg' and
'Fe'. So, there are two more cases here:
H faces J and both have neither 'Mg' nor 'Tc'. G
sits second to the right of J. Thus, cases 1, 2 and
2a are invalid.
